definition: The lawful conditionality by which, when this is, that comes to be and, with its quenching, that ceases.
notes: OSF editorial authority now treats Buddhadasa Bhikkhu as primary for this term. Conditionality remains the default because it is the cleanest reusable English rendering, but the repository now treats idappaccayatā as a core OSF doctrinal key rather than a minor supporting technicality. The term should govern the four standard conditionality lines rather than sit beside them as a loose abstraction. Translators should therefore keep idappaccayatā, paccaya, and the imasmiṃ sati / imassa nirodhā formulas in one coherent voice.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose focused on the morphology of the term
context_rules.notes: Use when the idiom of this conditioned by this is central.

context_rules.context: In Buddhadasa-style explanation of natural law
context_rules.notes: Notes may explicitly describe it as the natural law behind dependent arising.

context_rules.context: In formula-focused discussion of the four standard conditionality lines
context_rules.notes: Use the same voice as the companion formula records.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose focused on the literal structure of this conditioned by this
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when the morphology matters.

example_phrases.translation: when this is, this comes to be
with the quenching of this, this ceases
example_phrases.notes: Companion formula showing the quenching side of conditionality.

authority_basis.scope: Named in notes as support for the current idappaccayatā policy.
Supports treating Buddhadasa as primary guidance for dependent-arising and conditionality language in this doctrinal family.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'conditionality' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not separate this entry from the standard imasmiṃ sati and imassa nirodhā formulas when working in dependent-arising contexts.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents idappaccayatā from being treated as an abstract side-note instead of the governing conditionality behind the formula family.
